steps.title: Spot the passage's three-part structure
steps.content:
The passage opens with the novel's critical reception, then explains a complicated history of drafts and versions, and closes by describing how translators responded to this history.

The strongest signal: the passage builds logically—the complicated history (drafts, missing versions) directly explains why the translators needed to draw from multiple sources.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer
steps.content:
The correct answer describes exactly this three-part movement: it notes the critical reception, addresses the complicated development history, and shows how that history informed the translators' work.

The other choices either invent unsupported claims (B's declining reputation over time, C's assertion that the translation is superior to the original) or misrepresent what the translators actually did (D claims they selected one particular version when they actually drew from multiple sources to create a comprehensive edition).

That is the correct answer.
